schema_amendments.description: Boolean gate: true only when all entries in principle_10_audit have derivation_path anchored at published live edges or IR axioms.

Array tracking derivation path for every constant or structural condition introduced in this paper. I-9 Step 4 amended: paths must terminate at (a) a live edge from a published paper AI layer, or (b) an IR rule applied to a mathematical axiom. Loops or P2-only-anchored paths cause principle_10_compliant to remain false.

paper_title: Derivation and Universality: The β=1/2 Critical Exponent as a Universal Law

phase_ready.note: CBT I-9 all 7 steps PASS. Session S48. All three placeholders resolved S48: PH-2.3 (P3 C-3.REG), PH-2.4-EMPIRICAL (P3 C-3.REG), P10-GAP-2.5 (P3 C-3.σ). Two Thomas-review flags: derivation table S3 (RG flow argument) and S10 (eigenvalue normalisation) — pre-submission transparency markers, not invalidity flags.

cbt_steps.step_3_derivation: PASS (2 Thomas-review flags: S3 RG flow, S10 eigenvalue normalisation)

principle_10_compliant_note: All 5 gaps closed. P10-GAP-2.5 closed S48 via P3 C-3.σ Step R6 (bootstrap residuals, Bevington & Robinson 2003). Full Principle 10 compliance achieved.

principle_10_audit.derivation_path: P1 AI layer v11, Claim F-1.4 (Hopf quarter-wave theorem). β=1/2 derived for DDEs. P2 extends this to the universality class by RG argument. P2 is proving generality, not re-deriving the DDE result.

Derivation table S3→S7 via IR-9 (RG fixed-point), IR-5 (algebra), IR-8 (mode selection canonical n=0), IR-6 (equivalence). Derived from D-2.1(a),(b) without P1 F-1.4. Terminates at class criteria + IR axioms. Session S48 Phase 3.

(a) network topology — graph-theoretic axiom; (b) Hopf condition — bifurcation theory axiom (Guckenheimer & Holmes 1983 Ch.3); (c) measurement axiom — physical observability principle. All terminate at published mathematical axioms. Session S48 Phase 3.
Derivation table S8: β = Ω*τ/π = (π/2)/π = 1/2 by IR-3 (substitution of S7 result Ω*τ=π/2). Terminates at D-2.1 via S3→S7. Session S48 Phase 3.

Derivation table S10 (amplitude direction): eigenvalue = exp(−λ·τ_RG) < 1 for λ>0. λ>0 from P1 scope_boundary (μ<0 sub-critical — LIVE EDGE P1 AI layer v11). Terminates at P1 live edge + IR-11. Session S48 Phase 3.

principle_10_audit.constant_or_condition: C-2.4 threshold — 2σ comparability with P1 F-1.3
principle_10_audit.derivation_path: C-3.σ Step R6: σ_β computed from bootstrap residuals (B=1000 resamples, τ_gen fixed at structural value). 2σ = 95% CI of normal distribution. Anchored at Bevington & Robinson (2003) §3.2 standard error propagation. Universal statistical constant, not free parameter. IR-13 satisfied. Closes P10-GAP-2.5 carried from P2 CBT S48.

rules.name: Modus Ponens
rules.logical_form: If P and (P → Q), then Q.

rules.name: Universal Instantiation
rules.logical_form: If ∀x P(x), then P(a) for any specific a.

rules.name: Substitution of Equals
rules.logical_form: If a=b, then any expression containing a may replace a with b.

rules.name: Definition Expansion
rules.logical_form: Replace a defined term with its definition.

rules.name: Algebraic Manipulation
rules.logical_form: Valid algebraic transformation preserving equality or inequality.

rules.name: Logical Equivalence
rules.logical_form: Replace an expression with a logically equivalent one.

rules.name: Statistical Inference
rules.logical_form: Apply a named statistical test or estimation procedure to data.

rules.name: Parsimony / Modeling Principle Selection
rules.logical_form: Select a canonical value from a family of axiom-consistent options based on a named parsimony principle.

rules.name: RG Fixed-Point Identification
rules.logical_form: A fixed point of the RG transformation T is a point x* such that T(x*) = x*. The critical exponent β is determined by the eigenvalue spectrum of dT/dx at x*.
rules.justification: IR-9 added because IR-1–IR-8 do not contain a rule for fixed-point identification in a dynamical system. The fixed-point identification is a named mathematical procedure from renormalization group theory (Wilson 1971).

rules.name: Universality Class Identification
rules.logical_form: Two systems belong to the same universality class if and only if they share the same RG fixed point structure (same fixed point and same eigenvalue spectrum at that fixed point).
rules.justification: IR-10 added because universality class membership requires a specific logical structure (shared RG fixed point) that cannot be reduced to IR-2 without circularity. Standard RG definition (Kadanoff 1966, Wilson 1971).

rules.name: Perturbation Stability Argument
rules.logical_form: A fixed point x* is stable under perturbation ε if all eigenvalues of dT/dx at x* have magnitude < 1 in the RG flow sense.
rules.justification: IR-11 added because stability of the fixed point is a distinct logical step from identifying it (IR-9). Stability requires showing the eigenvalue magnitude condition separately.

inbound_dependencies.status: LIVE — P1 PHASE-READY, AI layer v12 published on GitHub
inbound_dependencies.note: P2 derives β=1/2 independently from the universality class criteria — NOT by re-running F-1.4. F-1.4 is confirmation that the FRM is within the class, not the proof of universality.

claim_registry.name: FRM Universality Class — Structural Definition
claim_registry.statement: The FRM universality class is the set of dynamical systems satisfying all three of the following structural criteria: (a) TOPOLOGY: the system exhibits delayed negative feedback with a single dominant delay τ > 0; (b) LINEARISATION: the dynamics near the critical point are governed by a characteristic equation with a conjugate pair of roots crossing the imaginary axis at the Hopf bifurcation; (c) TIMESCALE: τ is independently measurable and not inferred from the oscillation period.
derivation_sources: Network topology (criterion a): graph-theoretic property of the feedback architecture
Bifurcation theory (criterion b): standard Hopf condition on the characteristic equation (Guckenheimer & Holmes 1983 Ch.3)
Measurement axiom (criterion c): upstream measurement principle
claim_registry.principle_10_note: Criteria (a)–(c) are structural properties derivable from network topology and bifurcation theory. None of the criteria presuppose β=1/2.

claim_registry.name: β=1/2 Derivation Validity
claim_registry.statement: β=1/2 is the universal critical exponent for systems in the FRM universality class. This follows necessarily from the RG fixed-point analysis applied to the class definition D-2.1. The derivation is step-indexed and each step is valid under IR-1–IR-11.
claim_registry.vacuity_witness: Derivation table Step S1 output: expanded class definition (a),(b),(c) — non-null, non-trivial.

falsification_predicate.WHERE: n_invalid_steps: INTEGER — count of steps in P2 Section 3 derivation table where output does not follow from inputs under named rule IR-X. rg_fixed_point_exponent: SCALAR — value of beta produced by RG fixed-point analysis from D-2.1 without importing P1 F-1.4 result. class_definition_contains_stipulated_constant: BOOLEAN — TRUE if any constant in D-2.1(a)-(c) is absent from principle_10_audit with non-null derivation_path.
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Trace P2 Section 3 step by step. For each step: verify output follows from inputs by named IR rule. Record n_invalid_steps. Record final output as rg_fixed_point_exponent. Scan principle_10_audit for null derivation_path entries in D-2.1 constants.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: A step is invalid IFF output does not follow from inputs under named rule as stated. Invalid per se: (1) circular steps assuming beta=1/2; (2) steps introducing undefined variables; (3) steps appealing to unlisted rules. rg_fixed_point_exponent != 0.5 falsifies independently of n_invalid_steps.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-2.1. Type B-1 derivation validity. Threshold: n_invalid_steps = 0 is the only passing value — logical necessity, not statistical threshold. DDE-independence mandate: Ω*=π/2 must follow from D-2.1 without P1 F-1.4 (HR-3.1 mandate). Vacuity witness: derivation table S1 output.

claim_registry.name: Universality Class Membership
claim_registry.statement: The FRM belongs to the universality class characterised by β=1/2 and functional form f(t) = B + A·exp(−λt)·cos(ωt+φ). Membership is determined by criteria D-2.1(a)–(c), not by analogy.
claim_registry.vacuity_witness: LIVE: frm_criterion_a_satisfied — P1 AI layer v12 frm_parameters.lambda.expression uses tau_gen as sole delay parameter.

falsification_predicate.WHERE: frm_fails_any_criterion: BOOLEAN — TRUE if FRM fails D-2.1(a), (b), or (c). Evaluated against P1 AI layer v12 live edge F-1.4. membership_criterion_is_circular: BOOLEAN — TRUE if any criterion in D-2.1(a)-(c) uses beta=1/2 as a defining input. beta_not_implied_by_criteria: BOOLEAN — TRUE if C-2.1 is FALSIFIED (logically downstream).
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Steps 1-3: verify D-2.1(a)(b)(c) against P1 AI layer v12. Step 4: scan D-2.1 text for beta/1/2/exponent in definitional role. Step 5: check C-2.1 verdict. Step 6: evaluate FALSIFIED IF.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: All three criteria jointly necessary. Circular IFF beta=1/2 appears as a defining property. Correct form: structural properties (a)-(c) stated; beta=1/2 derived from them by IR-9/IR-10.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-2.2. Type B-2 membership. Two conditions: (i) FRM satisfies criteria; (ii) criteria are non-circular. Both required. Vacuity witness LIVE.

claim_registry.name: Functional Form Universality
claim_registry.statement: Systems in the FRM universality class exhibit f(t) = B + A·exp(−λt)·cos(ωt+φ) with λ and ω determined by the same derived expressions as in P1.

falsification_predicate.WHERE: system_in_class_exhibits_different_scaling: BOOLEAN — TRUE if any D-2.1-satisfying system exhibits different lambda/omega scaling. CROSS-PAPER PLACEHOLDER PH-2.3: pending P3 C-3.REG. lambda_omega_expressions_not_universal: BOOLEAN — TRUE if lambda=|alpha|/(Gamma*tau) and omega=pi/(2*tau) do not follow as corollaries of C-2.1 derivation.
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: For each verified substrate class, apply P3 C-3.REG to extract lambda and omega. Compare against derived expressions. Verify expressions appear in C-2.1 derivation table as corollaries.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: Gamma = 1+pi^2/4 DERIVED (P1 live edge). tau independently measured (D-2.1(c)). alpha = bifurcation distance (P1 scope_boundary). No stipulated constants.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-2.3. Extends P1 functional form to all class members. Downstream of C-2.1 and P3 C-3.REG. PH-2.3 non-blocking at PHASE-READY.

claim_registry.name: Substrate Independence (resolves PH-1.1)
claim_registry.statement: The critical exponent β=1/2 holds across all substrates satisfying the class membership criteria D-2.1(a)–(c), not only for DDEs.

falsification_predicate.WHERE: any_substrate_class_mean_beta_outside_2sigma: BOOLEAN — TRUE if mean fitted beta for any D-2.1-satisfying substrate class lies outside 0.5 +/- 2*sigma_S. Classes: (1) Kuramoto networks; (2) gene regulatory networks; (3) neural circuits with delayed inhibition. PLACEHOLDER PH-2.4-EMPIRICAL: pending P3 C-3.REG. substrate_class_fails_d21_AND_cited_as_counterexample: BOOLEAN — TRUE if a system failing D-2.1 is cited as a counterexample to β=1/2.
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: Step 1: verify D-2.1(a)-(c) for each substrate class. Step 2: apply C-3.REG to extract beta per system. Step 3: compute mean and sigma per class. Step 4: check 2*sigma bound.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: 2-sigma threshold: inherited from P1 F-1.3 for comparability. Limitation registered as P10-GAP-2.5. A system failing D-2.1 is not a counterexample.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-2.4 resolves PH-1.1 in P1 AI layer v12. Analytic component established by C-2.1+C-2.2; empirical pending P3 C-3.REG.

claim_registry.name: RG Fixed-Point Stability
claim_registry.statement: The β=1/2 fixed point is stable under perturbation of the class membership conditions. Shift in β is O(ε) for perturbation of size ε.

falsification_predicate.WHERE: beta_shift_exceeds_order_epsilon: BOOLEAN — TRUE if |beta(epsilon) - 0.5| grows faster than linearly in epsilon as epsilon->0. fixed_point_eigenvalue_exceeds_unity: BOOLEAN — TRUE if any eigenvalue of dT/dx at x*=0.5 has magnitude >= 1 in the amplitude direction.
falsification_predicate.EVALUATION: From derivation table S10: amplitude direction eigenvalue = exp(−λ·τ_RG). Verify λ>0 from P1 scope_boundary. Perturb D-2.1 by epsilon, compute beta(epsilon), evaluate Δβ/ε as ε->0.
falsification_predicate.BOUNDARY: ALL amplitude-direction eigenvalues must be < 1. Single eigenvalue >= 1 falsifies. Stability is of the amplitude envelope, not the phase angle.
falsification_predicate.CONTEXT: C-2.5. Standard RG stability criterion (IR-11). Without stability, β=1/2 holds only for fine-tuned systems.

steps_summary.output: θ*=π/2 (fundamental mode n=0, canonical choice documented)
All D-2.1 systems share FRM universality class with β=1/2
Fixed point stable: amplitude direction eigenvalue = exp(−λ·τ_RG) < 1

thomas_review_flags.description: RG flow from substrate-general G(iΩ,τ) to scalar exponential fixed-point form. Argument basis: Coullet & Spiegel (1983) amplitude equations. Thomas to review before journal submission.
Eigenvalue normalisation: stability is of the amplitude direction (exp(−λτ_RG) < 1 for λ>0), not the phase angle (eigenvalue π/2 in unnormalised units). Thomas to verify before journal submission.
